what_it_does: Records a posted note in which Tim reacts to a change-request/Accept/Reject idea and proposes expanding it with visual indicators for proposed updates, replies, and questions on the blocks he is commenting on.
observations: It is a noticeboard note with state posted, authored by tim in the dragnet-development channel.
It links to item-8c087208 with a commented_on relationship.
The body quotes a prior intent about members returning proposed diffs and Tim getting an Accept/Reject surface.
Tim asks for visual indicators when blocks he is commenting on receive proposed updates/changes or replies/questions.
The indicators are described as colors or icons, visible but not screen-consuming.
The note frames the interaction within a git-revert-is-the-safety posture.
standouts: Concrete UX proposal: typed visual indicators tied to active comment blocks.
Explicit connection to an Accept/Reject change-request loop and a prior board item.
Specific non-functional requirement: indicators must be noticeable yet not screen-eating.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML schema with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and timestamps.
Uses a markdown body prefixed with [point] and an inline quotation of the referenced idea.
Uses board:// URI addressing for items and links.
concerns: The term 'types' is used without defining whether it means indicator types, notification types, or comment types.
The proposal does not specify where indicators appear, how many states they cover, or interaction behavior.
The referenced 'Concept' and prior item are not included, so the full context is missing from this file alone.
notes: Tim's feedback note proposing glanceable, typed visual indicators on active comment blocks within an Accept/Reject change-request workflow.
questions: What exactly are the 'blocks' Tim is currently commenting on—code blocks, design regions, or board items?
What is the intended taxonomy of indicator 'types' and what state each represents?
How do the proposed indicators relate to the Accept/Reject surface mentioned earlier?
What is the content of item-8c087208 that this note comments on?
Are the indicators per-block, per-thread, or per-user?
purpose_vs_actual: The title frames it as a 'point' note, and it does record a point; however, the content is more prescriptive than observational, making it a feature/UX suggestion rather than a simple note.
intention_signals: To expand an Accept/Reject change-request concept by adding persistent, typed visual indicators on the blocks an author is commenting on, so proposed updates, replies, and questions are noticeable without consuming screen space.
novelty: It treats visual indicators as semantically typed events—proposed update/change versus reply/question—within a diff-acceptance workflow, rather than as generic unread badges.
contribution: Captures a user-level requirement for subtle, typed visual indicators on active comment blocks, preserving the specific design constraints (colors/icons, visible but not screen-eating) and linking them to an Accept/Reject change-request loop grounded in git-revert safety.
summary_for_embedding: A posted noticeboard note by Tim in dragnet-development that comments on item-8c087208 and proposes typed visual indicators for proposed updates, replies, and questions on blocks currently being commented on within an Accept/Reject change-request workflow.
intention_for_embedding: To expand a change-request/Accept/Reject concept with glanceable, non-intrusive visual indicators so an author can see when commented blocks have proposed updates, replies, or questions.
